basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Sajda — “The Prostration” — opens on the sending down of the Book “wherein is no doubt, from the Lord of the worlds,” against the charge of forgery. It unfolds the creation of the heavens and the earth in six days, the establishing upon the Throne, the governing of the affair which descends from heaven to earth then ascends back to Him. It celebrates the excellence of the creation of man: drawn first from clay, then from a despised fluid, then quickened by the breath of the divine Spirit — a paradox Qushayrī sums up thus: man is created from clay, and yet “He loves them and they love Him”. At the heart of the sūra shines the portrait of the true believers who, when reminded of the signs, “fall down prostrate” and glorify their Lord; their sides forsake their beds to call upon their Lord “in fear and longing,” for the night is the time of the beloved: for the lovers in the intimacy of nearness it is shorter than a glance. To these vigil-keepers “no soul knows what comfort of the eyes is kept hidden.” Prostration here is the very secret of nearness, and the sūra ceaselessly opposes the united believer to the separated transgressor, sealing on the command of a withdrawal that is wholly turned toward God.
themes: the descent of the Book without doubt
creation and the establishing upon the Throne
man of clay quickened by the Spirit
the prostration of the true believers
the night vigil and the hidden reward

versets.ar: الم
versets.tr: alif lām mīm
versets.trad: Alif. Lām. Mīm.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī deciphers the letters as a secret of love. The Alif points to His nearness: the lovers are smitten with it and cannot bear to be far from Him; the knowers read in it His glorification and find no intimacy save in Him. The Lām signals the meeting (liqāʾ) He holds in reserve for His beloved. The Mīm is the surrender by His intimates of their own will to His: for this He has preferred them above all His servants. The three letters condense the whole journey: smitten, awaited meeting, effacement of the self.

mots.def: opening letters; a cipher of love per Qushayrī.

versets.ar: تَنْزِيلُ الْكِتَابِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ مِنْ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: tanzīlu l-kitābi lā rayba fīhi min rabbi l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: The sending down of the Book, wherein is no doubt, is from the Lord of the worlds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the Book the letter of the beloved. When the meeting of loved ones becomes impossible, the most precious thing in their eyes is the letter of the Beloved. I sent down My Book upon My beloved, I bore to them My message as discourse. And he adds a tender paradox: it matters little to them that My reproach strikes their ears, for they are safe from My chastisement. The descent “wherein is no doubt” is thus the certainty of a word addressed from lover to lover.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ك ت ب
mots.sens: the sending down of the Book

mots.racine: ر ي ب
ر ب ب / ع ل م
mots.sens: from the Lord of the worlds
mots.def: rabb al-ʿālamīn: Lord of the universes.

versets.ar: أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَرَاهُ ۚ بَلْ هُوَ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّكَ لِتُنْذِرَ قَوْمًا مَا أَتَاهُمْ مِنْ نَذِيرٍ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: am yaqūlūna ftarāhu bal huwa l-ḥaqqu min rabbika li-tundhira qawman mā atāhum min nadhīrin min qablika laʿallahum yahtadūna
versets.trad: Or do they say: “He has forged it”? No! It is the Truth from your Lord, that you may warn a people to whom no warner came before you. Perhaps they will be guided.
versets.commentaire: To the enemies the message may seem suspect, but this touches the beloved not at all. Qushayrī: what comes to you from Us is for you a reality; if it remains obscure to the adversaries, it neither harms you nor weighs upon you. Then this touch of intimacy: the heart's converse with the Beloved is never sweeter than when it is joined to the absence of any watcher. The slander of the deniers only seals the secret between the lover and his Lord.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ف ر ي
mots.sens: do they say: he forged it?

mots.racine: ح ق ق
ن ذ ر / ق و م
mots.sens: that you may warn a people

mots.racine: ه د ي

versets.ar: اللَّهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ اسْتَوَى عَلَى الْعَرْشِ ۖ مَا لَكُمْ مِنْ دُونِهِ مِنْ وَلِيٍّ وَلَا شَفِيعٍ ۚ أَفَلَا تَتَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: allāhu lladhī khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa wa-mā baynahumā fī sittati ayyāmin thumma stawā ʿalā l-ʿarshi mā lakum min dūnihi min waliyyin wa-lā shafīʿin a-fa-lā tatadhakkarūna
versets.trad: God it is who created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days, then established Himself upon the Throne. You have, apart from Him, neither protector nor intercessor. Will you not be reminded?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī frees time from any hold over the Creator. These days He created from what is not the day: it is not required that the created come to be in time, since time itself was created outside of time. As for the Throne: the One established upon the Throne is the Eternal without limit; no nearness or distance by essence concerns Him. And the Throne, for all its glory, remains inert: had it life, it would be the most parched of beings for a single splinter of union — but the Throne is matter, and matter knows no desire. Apart from Him, neither ally nor intercessor: if He wills you no good, no sky shelters you, no earth bears you without His good pleasure.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: He created the heavens and the earth

mots.racine: س ت ت / ي و م
س و ي / ع ر ش
mots.sens: then established Himself upon the Throne
mots.def: istawā: to establish in majesty; ʿarsh: Throne.

mots.racine: و ل ي / ش ف ع
ذ ك ر

versets.ar: يُدَبِّرُ الْأَمْرَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ ثُمَّ يَعْرُجُ إِلَيْهِ فِي يَوْمٍ كَانَ مِقْدَارُهُ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ مِمَّا تَعُدُّونَ
versets.tr: yudabbiru l-amra mina s-samāʾi ilā l-arḍi thumma yaʿruju ilayhi fī yawmin kāna miqdāruhu alfa sanatin mimmā taʿuddūna
versets.trad: He directs the affair from the heaven to the earth, then it ascends to Him in a Day whose measure is a thousand years of what you count.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He addresses creatures to the measure of their understanding and of what they can grasp, through the realities to which their speech has accustomed them. A thousand years of what you count: the human measure of time is but a concession to the weakness of comprehension, for the divine affair descends and ascends according to an order that escapes all measurable duration.

mots.racine: د ب ر / أ م ر
س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: from the heaven to the earth
mots.def: the descending path of the affair.

mots.racine: ع ر ج
أ ل ف / س ن و / ع د د
mots.sens: a thousand years of what you count

versets.ar: ذَلِكَ عَالِمُ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ الْعَزِيزُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: dhālika ʿālimu l-ghaybi wa-sh-shahādati l-ʿazīzu r-raḥīmu
versets.trad: Such is the Knower of the unseen and the seen, the Mighty, the Compassionate,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī weds the two Names in a subtle balance. He is the Mighty (al-ʿazīz) toward the obedient and the Compassionate (al-raḥīm) toward the rebellious. And he reverses the gaze: Mighty toward the obedient, to break their pretension; Compassionate toward the rebellious, to lift their fault. Thus might humbles whoever would think himself great by his obedience, and mercy raises whoever sin would have cast down.

mots.racine: ع ل م / غ ي ب / ش ه د
mots.sens: Knower of the unseen and the seen

mots.racine: ع ز ز
ر ح م

versets.ar: الَّذِي أَحْسَنَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ خَلَقَهُ ۖ وَبَدَأَ خَلْقَ الْإِنْسَانِ مِنْ طِينٍ
versets.tr: alladhī aḥsana kulla shayʾin khalaqahu wa-badaʾa khalqa l-insāni min ṭīnin
versets.trad: Who made beautiful everything He created, and began the creation of man from clay;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contemplates the beauty spread over every creature: the Throne is a red ruby, the angels have wings in twos, threes and fours, Gabriel is the peacock of the angels, the houris are of matchless grace, and the Gardens as the reports describe. Then, reaching man, the text says only: “from clay.” And there lies the mystery: so many beauties elsewhere — and yet it is upon you, O one made of clay, that My choice has fallen. For man is drawn from mire, and of him it is said: “He loves them and they love Him.”

mots.racine: ح س ن / خ ل ق
mots.sens: He made beautiful all He created

mots.racine: ب د أ / خ ل ق / أ ن س
mots.sens: and began the creation of man
mots.def: badaʾa: to begin; insān: the human.

mots.racine: ط ي ن

versets.ar: ثُمَّ جَعَلَ نَسْلَهُ مِنْ سُلَالَةٍ مِنْ مَاءٍ مَهِينٍ
versets.tr: thumma jaʿala naslahu min sulālatin min māʾin mahīnin
versets.trad: Then He made his progeny from an extract of a despised fluid;
versets.commentaire: Man drawn from a despised drop — and yet, Qushayrī continues, it is of this creature of mire that it is said: “Remember Me, and I will remember you.” He strings the paradoxes together: man is created from clay, and yet “God is well pleased with them and they with Him.” The humbleness of the origin does not contradict the election: it is precisely from the lowest that God brings forth the most beloved.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ن س ل
س ل ل
م و ه / م ه ن

versets.ar: ثُمَّ سَوَّاهُ وَنَفَخَ فِيهِ مِنْ رُوحِهِ ۖ وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ السَّمْعَ وَالْأَبْصَارَ وَالْأَفْئِدَةَ ۚ قَلِيلًا مَا تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: thumma sawwāhu wa-nafakha fīhi min rūḥihi wa-jaʿala lakumu s-samʿa wa-l-abṣāra wa-l-afʾidata qalīlan mā tashkurūna
versets.trad: then He fashioned him in due proportion and breathed into him of His spirit. And He made for you hearing, sight and hearts. Little do you give thanks!
versets.commentaire: Here is the summit of the paradox of clay: kneaded from mire, man yet receives the very breath of the divine Spirit. Qushayrī had prepared this vertigo — created from clay, and yet “He loves them and they love Him” — and the inbreathing seals it: the humble clay becomes bearer of the secret. Yet hearing, sight, the heart, these royal gifts, remain little acknowledged: “little do you give thanks!” Ingratitude is the only denial the creature opposes to so much election.

mots.racine: س و ي
mots.sens: then He fashioned him in proportion

mots.racine: ن ف خ / ر و ح
mots.sens: and breathed into him of His spirit

mots.racine: س م ع / ب ص ر / ف أ د
mots.sens: the hearing, the sight, the hearts

mots.racine: ق ل ل / ش ك ر

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا أَإِذَا ضَلَلْنَا فِي الْأَرْضِ أَإِنَّا لَفِي خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ ۚ بَلْ هُمْ بِلِقَاءِ رَبِّهِمْ كَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū a-idhā ḍalalnā fī l-arḍi a-innā la-fī khalqin jadīdin bal hum bi-liqāʾi rabbihim kāfirūna
versets.trad: And they say: “When we are lost in the earth, shall we indeed be in a new creation?” Rather, they are disbelievers in the meeting with their Lord.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī probes the root of their refusal: had they but a single particle of gnosis, a breath of yearning, a waft of love, never would they so obstinately deny the possibility of return to God. Their denial of resurrection is not an error of reasoning but a dryness of heart: “they are disbelievers in the meeting with their Lord.” Whoever loves the meeting cannot deny the return that makes it possible.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / أ ر ض
mots.sens: when we are lost in the earth
mots.def: ḍalla: to be lost, to dissolve.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ج د د
mots.sens: shall we be in a new creation?

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ر ب ب / ك ف ر

versets.ar: قُلْ يَتَوَفَّاكُمْ مَلَكُ الْمَوْتِ الَّذِي وُكِّلَ بِكُمْ ثُمَّ إِلَى رَبِّكُمْ تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: qul yatawaffākum malaku l-mawti lladhī wukkila bikum thumma ilā rabbikum turjaʿūna
versets.trad: Say: “The angel of death who is charged with you will take you; then to your Lord you will be returned.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī pierces the veil of the secondary cause. Were it not for the heedlessness of their hearts, the taking of their souls would never have been ascribed to the angel of death, for the angel has of himself no hold over anyone, no power of his own: what occurs in the taking of souls belongs to the properties of the Real's power alone. But because they are distracted from contemplating the Lord, God speaks to them to the measure of their understanding and suspends their hearts upon intermediaries. “Then to your Lord you will be returned”: behind the angel, it is always to Him that one returns.

mots.racine: و ف ي / م ل ك / م و ت
mots.sens: the angel of death takes you
mots.def: tawaffā: to take the soul; malak al-mawt: angel of death.

mots.racine: و ك ل
ر ب ب / ر ج ع
mots.sens: then to your Lord you are returned

versets.ar: وَلَوْ تَرَى إِذِ الْمُجْرِمُونَ نَاكِسُو رُؤُوسِهِمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ رَبَّنَا أَبْصَرْنَا وَسَمِعْنَا فَارْجِعْنَا نَعْمَلْ صَالِحًا إِنَّا مُوقِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law tarā idhi l-mujrimūna nākisū ruʾūsihim ʿinda rabbihim rabbanā abṣarnā wa-samiʿnā fa-rjiʿnā naʿmal ṣāliḥan innā mūqinūna
versets.trad: If only you could see when the guilty hang their heads before their Lord: “Our Lord! We have seen and we have heard. Send us back: we will do good — we are now certain!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the scene in two brief and terrible strokes: stupor has seized them, shame has overwhelmed them; they make excuses when it is no longer the time for excuse, they confess when confession no longer avails. The “we have seen and heard” that would have saved them in life is now, at the threshold of judgment, only a regret without ransom. Certainty come too late is the bitterest of chastisements: to see clearly when the way of return is shut.

mots.racine: ن ك س / ر أ س
ب ص ر / س م ع
mots.def: abṣara: to see; samiʿa: to hear.

mots.racine: ر ج ع / ع م ل / ص ل ح
mots.sens: send us back, we will do good

mots.racine: ي ق ن

versets.ar: وَلَوْ شِئْنَا لَآتَيْنَا كُلَّ نَفْسٍ هُدَاهَا وَلَكِنْ حَقَّ الْقَوْلُ مِنِّي لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ
versets.tr: wa-law shiʾnā la-ātaynā kulla nafsin hudāhā wa-lākin ḥaqqa l-qawlu minnī la-amlaʾanna jahannama mina l-jinnati wa-n-nāsi ajmaʿīna
versets.trad: Had We willed, We would have given every soul its guidance. But the word from Me has come true: “I shall surely fill Hell with jinn and men together.”
versets.commentaire: Here Qushayrī unfolds a dizzying commentary on predestination, yet mingled with love. Had We willed, We would have smoothed the path of proof and perpetuated the assistance for everyone; but Our will attached itself to the leading-astray of one people just as it attached itself to the drawing-near of another. And he then addresses the servant: O wretched one, you have spent your life in toil and pain, you have multiplied your striving — but what can you change in My decree? With what power would you reverse it? Yet the decree, he adds, remains “bound to the will of the Creator”: a predestination steeped in love.

mots.ar: وَلَوْ شِئْنَا لَآتَيْنَا كُلَّ نَفْسٍ هُدَاهَا
mots.tr: wa-law shiʾnā la-ātaynā kulla nafsin hudāhā
mots.racine: ش ي أ / أ ت ي / ن ف س / ه د ي
mots.sens: had We willed We would give each soul its guidance

mots.racine: ح ق ق / ق و ل
mots.sens: the word from Me came true
mots.def: ḥaqqa: to be verified, to come true.

mots.racine: م ل أ

versets.ar: فَذُوقُوا بِمَا نَسِيتُمْ لِقَاءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هَذَا إِنَّا نَسِينَاكُمْ ۖ وَذُوقُوا عَذَابَ الْخُلْدِ بِمَا كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-dhūqū bimā nasītum liqāʾa yawmikum hādhā innā nasīnākum wa-dhūqū ʿadhāba l-khuldi bimā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: So taste, for having forgotten the meeting of this your Day — We too have forgotten you. Taste the chastisement of eternity for what you used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses the sentence into one cutting formula: endure the humiliation your disobedience has earned, and abide forever in the abode of disgrace for the unbelief you have amassed. The “forgetting” with which God repays forgetting is no lapse of knowledge but abandonment: they forsook the meeting, they are in turn forsaken. The taste of chastisement answers exactly the taste they took in heedlessness.

mots.racine: ذ و ق / ن س ي
mots.def: dhāqa: to taste; nasiya: to forget.

mots.racine: ن س ي
ع ذ ب / خ ل د

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا يُؤْمِنُ بِآيَاتِنَا الَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِّرُوا بِهَا خَرُّوا سُجَّدًا وَسَبَّحُوا بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ وَهُمْ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ ۩
versets.tr: innamā yuʾminu bi-āyātinā lladhīna idhā dhukkirū bihā kharrū sujjadan wa-sabbaḥū bi-ḥamdi rabbihim wa-hum lā yastakbirūna
versets.trad: Only those believe in Our signs who, when reminded of them, fall down prostrate and glorify the praise of their Lord, and they are not arrogant.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opposes faith and denial as two irreconcilable contraries: denial is rejection and arrogance, assent is prostration and realization; whoever is clothed in one sees the other effaced in him. Then he doubles the prostration: they prostrate by their outward forms in the prayer-niche, and in their secrets upon the dust of humility, on the carpet of submission, marked with effacement and struck with extinction. And this luminous question: how should he be arrogant who finds the fullness of his rest and the truth of his intimacy only in his self-abasement before his Adored?

mots.racine: أ م ن / أ ي ي
mots.sens: only those believe in Our signs

mots.racine: خ ر ر / س ج د
mots.def: kharra: to fall; sujjad: prostrate ones.

mots.racine: س ب ح / ح م د / ر ب ب
mots.sens: and glorify the praise of their Lord

mots.racine: ك ب ر

versets.ar: تَتَجَافَى جُنُوبُهُمْ عَنِ الْمَضَاجِعِ يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُمْ خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ يُنْفِقُونَ
versets.tr: tatajāfā junūbuhum ʿani l-maḍājiʿi yadʿūna rabbahum khawfan wa-ṭamaʿan wa-mimmā razaqnāhum yunfiqūna
versets.trad: Their sides forsake their beds, calling upon their Lord in fear and longing, and of what We have provided them they spend.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the verse on two levels. Outwardly: they leave the bed to fulfill the right of worship and the night vigil; inwardly: their hearts withdraw from complacency in states, from contemplating their own effort, from the illusion of station — for all this veils the Reality and remains for the servant a deadly poison. And he celebrates the night as the time of the lovers: night is the time of the beloved; day is the time of the people of this world. For the lovers it is now the joy of meeting, now the war of separation: if they are in the intimacy of nearness, their night is shorter than a glance; and if they are in the trial of distance, their night is endless.

mots.racine: ج ف و / ج ن ب / ض ج ع
د ع و / خ و ف / ط م ع
mots.sens: calling their Lord in fear and longing

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ن ف ق
mots.sens: and of what We provide them they spend
mots.def: razaqa: to provide; anfaqa: to spend.

versets.ar: فَلَا تَعْلَمُ نَفْسٌ مَا أُخْفِيَ لَهُمْ مِنْ قُرَّةِ أَعْيُنٍ جَزَاءً بِمَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lā taʿlamu nafsun mā ukhfiya lahum min qurrati aʿyunin jazāʾan bimā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: No soul knows what comfort of the eyes is kept hidden for them, as a reward for what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the “coolness of the eyes” the vision of the Beloved. The eye finds coolness only in the sight of whom one loves, or of what one loves; so watch over your heart and heed your state, for today's joy will be prolonged tomorrow, and in the state you are in you will be raised. He then cites the ḥadīth: “Whoever is in a state will meet God in that state.” The reserved happiness is so high that none can describe it: at most one may say that it is a precious state and a sublime quality.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ن ف س
خ ف ي
ق ر ر / ع ي ن
mots.def: qurrat al-ʿayn: joy, solace of the eye.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / ع م ل
mots.sens: as reward for what they did

versets.ar: أَفَمَنْ كَانَ مُؤْمِنًا كَمَنْ كَانَ فَاسِقًا ۚ لَا يَسْتَوُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-man kāna muʾminan ka-man kāna fāsiqan lā yastawūna
versets.trad: Is then one who is a believer like one who is a transgressor? They are not equal!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes the opposition of the believer and the transgressor into that of the united lover and the separated one, through a cascade of questions. Is he who, in union, trails his robes of glory like him who, in the abasement of separation, endures its torment? Is he who is in the breath of nearness like him who toils in the weariness of burden? Is he who is in the day of gnosis and the brightness of beneficence like him who is in the nights of denial and the desolation of rebellion? And he settles it: they are not equal, they do not meet!

mots.racine: أ م ن
mots.sens: is one who is a believer

mots.racine: ف س ق
mots.sens: like one who is a transgressor

mots.racine: س و ي

versets.ar: أَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ فَلَهُمْ جَنَّاتُ الْمَأْوَى نُزُلًا بِمَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: ammā lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti fa-lahum jannātu l-maʾwā nuzulan bimā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: As for those who believe and do righteous deeds, theirs are the Gardens of the Refuge as a dwelling, for what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sums up the promise in a cadence of bliss. “Those who believed”: they held it true; “and did righteous deeds”: they realized what they had affirmed — theirs is the beauty of state, the praise of becoming and the abundance of the gift. The Garden of the Refuge is not merely a reward for deeds, but the dwelling of the soul which, having believed and labored, regains its place of origin and its rest.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ع م ل / ص ل ح
ج ن ن / أ و ي
ن ز ل / ع م ل
mots.sens: as welcome for what they did

versets.ar: وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ فَسَقُوا فَمَأْوَاهُمُ النَّارُ ۖ كُلَّمَا أَرَادُوا أَنْ يَخْرُجُوا مِنْهَا أُعِيدُوا فِيهَا وَقِيلَ لَهُمْ ذُوقُوا عَذَابَ النَّارِ الَّذِي كُنْتُمْ بِهِ تُكَذِّبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ammā lladhīna fasaqū fa-maʾwāhumu n-nāru kullamā arādū an yakhrujū minhā uʿīdū fīhā wa-qīla lahum dhūqū ʿadhāba n-nāri lladhī kuntum bihi tukadhdhibūna
versets.trad: But as for those who transgressed, their refuge is the Fire. Whenever they wish to come out of it, they are returned into it, and it is said to them: “Taste the chastisement of the Fire which you used to deny!”
versets.commentaire: To the believers' beautiful return Qushayrī opposes the imprisonment of the transgressors: those who toiled and denied, who did evil and corrupted their dealings — their ultimate lot is disgrace and humiliation, a thousand kinds of trials and shades of pain. And he seals the verse with a hopeless image: whenever they seek deliverance from their torment, they sink the deeper into it; whenever they hope for salvation, they are made to drink an added measure of despair.

mots.racine: ف س ق / أ و ي / ن و ر
mots.sens: the transgressors, their refuge is the Fire

mots.ar: كُلَّمَا أَرَادُوا أَنْ يَخْرُجُوا مِنْهَا أُعِيدُوا فِيهَا
mots.tr: kullamā arādū an yakhrujū minhā uʿīdū fīhā
mots.racine: ر و د / خ ر ج / ع و د
mots.sens: whenever they wish to leave they are returned
mots.def: kullamā: whenever; uʿīda: to be returned.

mots.racine: ذ و ق / ع ذ ب
mots.sens: taste the chastisement of the Fire

versets.ar: وَلَنُذِيقَنَّهُمْ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ الْأَدْنَى دُونَ الْعَذَابِ الْأَكْبَرِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-nudhīqannahum mina l-ʿadhābi l-adnā dūna l-ʿadhābi l-akbari laʿallahum yarjiʿūna
versets.trad: And We shall surely make them taste of the nearer chastisement before the greater chastisement, that perhaps they may return.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī internalizes the two chastisements and gives several readings. For some, the nearer chastisement is the trials of this world, and the greater chastisement the torment of the hereafter. For others, the nearer chastisement is a languor that seizes them in their worship, and the greater chastisement a hardness that creeps over their hearts. For others still, the nearer chastisement is a halt in their wayfaring that turns them back, and the greater chastisement a veil that cuts them off from their contemplations. And he cites this verse: “You disciplined me by turning your heart from me — look upon me, then, for you have disciplined me well.”

mots.racine: ذ و ق / ع ذ ب / د ن و
mots.sens: We will make them taste the nearer chastisement

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ك ب ر
ر ج ع

versets.ar: وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنْ ذُكِّرَ بِآيَاتِ رَبِّهِ ثُمَّ أَعْرَضَ عَنْهَا ۚ إِنَّا مِنَ الْمُجْرِمِينَ مُنْتَقِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-man aẓlamu mimman dhukkira bi-āyāti rabbihi thumma aʿraḍa ʿanhā innā mina l-mujrimīna muntaqimūna
versets.trad: And who is more unjust than one who is reminded of the signs of his Lord, then turns away from them? Surely We shall take vengeance on the guilty.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī describes the fate of the warned soul that persists. When the servant is roused by every kind of admonition, shaken — for forsaking the bounds of fidelity — by sundry corrections, and yet does not desist from his conduct, deceived by the length of his respite, deeming himself safe from the assaults of the divine ruse and the secrets of His decree, then He seizes him suddenly, with no escape from His grasp. The worst of the unjust is he who, reminded, turns away: his very security is the snare.

mots.racine: ظ ل م

mots.ar: ذُكِّرَ بِآيَاتِ رَبِّهِ ثُمَّ أَعْرَضَ عَنْهَا
mots.tr: dhukkira bi-āyāti rabbihi thumma aʿraḍa ʿanhā
mots.racine: ذ ك ر / أ ي ي / ع ر ض
mots.sens: reminded of his Lord's signs then turns away

mots.racine: ج ر م / ن ق م
mots.sens: We will avenge Ourselves on the guilty
mots.def: intaqama: to take vengeance; mujrim: criminal.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْكِتَابَ فَلَا تَكُنْ فِي مِرْيَةٍ مِنْ لِقَائِهِ ۖ وَجَعَلْنَاهُ هُدًى لِبَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ātaynā mūsā l-kitāba fa-lā takun fī miryatin min liqāʾihi wa-jaʿalnāhu hudan li-banī isrāʾīla
versets.trad: We gave Moses the Book — so be not in doubt of His meeting — and We made it a guidance for the Children of Israel.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī refers “His meeting” to the meeting of God and His vision. Be not in doubt of His meeting tomorrow, nor of His vision for us. Then he draws a correspondence between the prophets: just as it was a guidance for the Children of Israel, here is Muḥammad — peace be upon him — made a mercy for the worlds. The Book given to Moses and the mercy sent through the Prophet are two rays of one same light.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ك ت ب

mots.ar: فَلَا تَكُنْ فِي مِرْيَةٍ مِنْ لِقَائِهِ
mots.tr: fa-lā takun fī miryatin min liqāʾihi
mots.racine: م ر ي / ل ق ي
mots.sens: be not in doubt of His meeting

mots.racine: ه د ي
mots.sens: guidance for the Children of Israel

versets.ar: وَجَعَلْنَا مِنْهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنَا لَمَّا صَبَرُوا ۖ وَكَانُوا بِآيَاتِنَا يُوقِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-jaʿalnā minhum aʾimmatan yahdūna bi-amrinā lammā ṣabarū wa-kānū bi-āyātinā yūqinūna
versets.trad: And We appointed from among them leaders who guided by Our command, when they were patient and had certainty in Our signs.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes patience the root of guidance. When they were patient in seeking Us, they were filled with Our presence, and what they received of Our favors overflowed onto those who followed them; the radiance of their suns spread over all their people. And he exalts them: they are guides for the creatures, living springs in religion, and stars for those who seek the way. The guidance of others is born of long endurance in the quest.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / أ م م
mots.sens: We appointed leaders from among them

mots.racine: ه د ي / أ م ر
ص ب ر
mots.def: ṣabara: to be patient, to endure.

mots.racine: أ ي ي / ي ق ن
mots.sens: in Our signs they had certainty

versets.ar: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ يَفْصِلُ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ فِيمَا كَانُوا فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ
versets.tr: inna rabbaka huwa yafṣilu baynahum yawma l-qiyāmati fīmā kānū fīhi yakhtalifūna
versets.trad: Surely your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that wherein they used to differ.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unveils what the judgment makes manifest: He decides between them, and then is distinguished the rejected from the accepted, the forsaken from the united, the contented from the led-astray, the enemy from the friend. And he seals it with an exclamation in which the whole stake trembles: how many joys will endure there without end! And how many souls will melt away in anguish at that moment! The Day that decides brings into open day the secret of each destiny.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ف ص ل
mots.sens: your Lord will decide between them

mots.racine: ي و م / ق و م
خ ل ف

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَهْدِ لَهُمْ كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ مِنَ الْقُرُونِ يَمْشُونَ فِي مَسَاكِنِهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ ۖ أَفَلَا يَسْمَعُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yahdi lahum kam ahlaknā min qablihim mina l-qurūni yamshūna fī masākinihim inna fī dhālika la-āyātin a-fa-lā yasmaʿūna
versets.trad: Has it not been a guidance to them how many generations We destroyed before them, in whose dwellings they now walk? In that are signs indeed. Will they not hear?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the ruins a silent homily. Have they not taken a lesson from the dwellings of peoples who were in splendor and became a warning, who were in joy and ended in ruin? All their houses and visited places have passed to others than themselves, their goods returned to their likes; they dwelt in their shadows without taking a lesson from those who, before them, were like them. To walk among the ruins of the perished without trembling is to be more deaf than stone.

mots.racine: ه د ي

mots.ar: كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ مِنَ الْقُرُونِ
mots.tr: kam ahlaknā min qablihim mina l-qurūni
mots.racine: ه ل ك / ق ر ن
mots.sens: how many generations We destroyed before them

mots.racine: م ش ي / س ك ن
س م ع

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّا نَسُوقُ الْمَاءَ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ الْجُرُزِ فَنُخْرِجُ بِهِ زَرْعًا تَأْكُلُ مِنْهُ أَنْعَامُهُمْ وَأَنْفُسُهُمْ ۖ أَفَلَا يُبْصِرُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yaraw annā nasūqu l-māʾa ilā l-arḍi l-juruzi fa-nukhriju bihi zarʿan taʾkulu minhu anʿāmuhum wa-anfusuhum a-fa-lā yubṣirūna
versets.trad: Have they not seen that We drive the water to the barren land and bring forth by it crops from which their cattle and they themselves eat? Will they not see?
themes: the heart's land made green again
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes the rain upon the dead land into grace upon the parched heart. The sign is this: We water the gardens of their union after their wood has dried and what was familiar to them has vanished from their habit; then their branch grows green again after its withering, telling by its state the return of their first meeting. The aridity of the heart is never final: the water of favor can make green again what was thought dead.

mots.racine: س و ق / م و ه / ج ر ز
mots.sens: We drive the water to the barren land
mots.def: sāqa: to drive; juruz: land without verdure.

mots.racine: خ ر ج / ز ر ع
mots.sens: We bring forth by it crops
mots.def: akhraja: to bring forth; zarʿ: sowing.

mots.racine: أ ك ل / ن ع م / ن ف س
mots.sens: from which their cattle and they eat

mots.racine: ب ص ر

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَى هَذَا الْفَتْحُ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlūna matā hādhā l-fatḥu in kuntum ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: And they say: “When is this victory, if you are truthful?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads in their mocking question the denial of the Day of meeting. They deemed improbable the Day of the face-to-face and denied it; so He informed them that, when they witness it, nothing will remain for them but bitterness and trial. What they demand in challenge as a proof of truthfulness will turn against them on the day the “victory” dawns: it will be their regret, not their triumph.

mots.racine: ق و ل
ف ت ح
ص د ق

versets.ar: قُلْ يَوْمَ الْفَتْحِ لَا يَنْفَعُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِيمَانُهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يُنْظَرُونَ
versets.tr: qul yawma l-fatḥi lā yanfaʿu lladhīna kafarū īmānuhum wa-lā hum yunẓarūna
versets.trad: Say: “On the Day of victory the faith of those who disbelieved will not avail them, nor will they be granted respite.”
versets.commentaire: The “Day of victory” is for Qushayrī the day of the meeting they denied. The faith that rises at the moment the veil falls no longer profits: it is no longer free assent but forced acknowledgment. And no respite will be granted them to mend — the delay they abused throughout their lives is now closed. What they demanded in scorn comes upon them as a sentence beyond appeal.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ف ت ح
ن ف ع / ك ف ر / أ م ن
mots.sens: the disbelievers' faith does not avail

mots.racine: ن ظ ر

versets.ar: فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ وَانْتَظِرْ إِنَّهُمْ مُنْتَظِرُونَ
versets.tr: fa-aʿriḍ ʿanhum wa-ntaẓir innahum muntaẓirūna
versets.trad: So turn away from them and wait! They too are waiting...
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī turns the command of withdrawal into a movement of love, in a finale of great beauty. Turn away from them by occupying yourself with Us, by turning toward Us, by withdrawing into Us alone. “And wait” for the increases of Our union and the gifts of Our kindness. “They too are waiting” for the assaults of Our wrath and the secrets of Our ruse — and soon each will find, present before him, what he was waiting for. The same waiting radically separates the fulfilled lover from the surprised denier.

mots.racine: ع ر ض
ن ظ ر
mots.def: intaẓara: to wait, to watch for.

mots.racine: ن ظ ر
